script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'agent-pipeline-deep-audit',
  description: 'Deep audit of the HC-GRC agent + pipeline code against their AGENT.md/SAP/protocol specs and research-integrity invariants (honest bar, adversarially verified)',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Audit', detail: 'one deep reviewer per agent/module + cross-cutting checks' },
    { title: 'Verify', detail: 'adversarially verify each finding' },
    { title: 'Synthesize', detail: 'collect confirmed findings' },
  ],
}

const REPO = '/Users/thomasjones/hc-grc'

const FINDINGS = {
  type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
  properties: {
    unit: { type: 'string' },
    findings: {
      type: 'array',
      items: {
        type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
        properties: {
          title: { type: 'string' },
          severity: { type: 'string', enum: ['low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical'] },
          location: { type: 'string' },
          problem: { type: 'string' },
          spec_or_invariant: { type: 'string', description: 'the AGENT.md/SAP/protocol clause or integrity invariant violated' },
          proposed_fix: { type: 'string' },
        },
        required: ['title', 'severity', 'location', 'problem', 'spec_or_invariant', 'proposed_fix'],
      },
    },
  },
  required: ['unit', 'findings'],
}

const VERDICT = {
  type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
  properties: {
    title: { type: 'string' },
    confirmed: { type: 'boolean' },
    adjusted_severity: { type: 'string', enum: ['none', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical'] },
    is_stub_by_design: { type: 'boolean', description: 'true if this is just an intentional Phase-0/1 NotImplementedError stub (IMPLEMENTED=False) and NOT a real defect' },
    verification: { type: 'string' },
    recommended_action: { type: 'string' },
  },
  required: ['title', 'confirmed', 'adjusted_severity', 'is_stub_by_design', 'verification', 'recommended_action'],
}

// Shared context every auditor gets.
const CONTEXT =
  `Repo ${REPO}. This is the HC-GRC research-governance platform: a LangGraph agency that empirically tests the Secure Controls Framework's STRM mappings under a 5-gate human-in-the-loop topology.\n\n` +
  `CRITICAL FRAMING — most agents are INTENTIONAL Phase-0/1 STUBS: their run()/run_exploratory()/run_confirmatory() raise NotImplementedError with class flag IMPLEMENTED=False. That is BY DESIGN (the real ML pipeline isn't live yet) and is NOT a defect — do NOT report "agent doesn't do the work yet". \n\n` +
  `Report GENUINE defects only, in these categories:\n` +
  `1. CORRECTNESS of logic that IS actually implemented (e.g. the test-split access checks in data_steward.get_split_paths run real gate-checking BEFORE the NotImplementedError; base.assert_gate2_approved; data_split seeding; any helper/dataclass logic; __init__ registries).\n` +
  `2. RESEARCH-INTEGRITY invariants (the highest stakes): the test-split firewall (no test access before the required gate(s)); EXP_ artifact prefix; NO p-value/decision language in exploratory outputs; SAP header on confirmatory; HARKing prevention; split-seed immutability after Gate 2.\n` +
  `3. SPEC MISALIGNMENT: where the code contradicts its own agents/<area>/<name>/AGENT.md role doc, docs/protocol/03_statistical_analysis_plan.md, docs/protocol/04_methods_scaffolding.md, docs/charter/, or the base.py contract. Find the AGENT.md via grep/find.\n` +
  `4. CONTRACT adherence: PROTECTED / IMPLEMENTED / AGENT_ID flags set correctly and consistently with base.py and the AGENT.md; return value is a partial-state dict (never mutates state in place); correct base class.\n` +
  `5. INCONSISTENCIES between modules (e.g. does base.assert_gate2_approved's Gate-2-only check contradict data_steward's Gate-2-AND-Gate-3 requirement? could a P-agent's run_confirmatory reach the test split through a weaker check?).\n\n` +
  `Read the REAL code and the REAL spec docs (Read/Grep/Bash read-only). Be precise with file:line, cite the exact spec clause. Honest severity: 'critical'/'high' only for real integrity/correctness defects reachable in practice; a doc/stub mismatch is usually low/medium. Return [] if the unit is genuinely sound. Do NOT invent issues.`

const AGENT_UNITS = [
  { key: 'base-contract', path: 'src/agents/base.py + src/agents/__init__.py', focus: 'the agent contract: SAPViolationError/assert_gate2_approved correctness, phase dispatch, PROTECTED/IMPLEMENTED semantics, the agent registry exports. Does assert_gate2_approved (Gate-2 only) under-enforce vs data_steward (Gate 2 AND Gate 3)?' },
  { key: 'data-acquisition', path: 'src/agents/data_acquisition/agent.py', focus: 'real logic vs stub; AGENT.md alignment; provenance; corpus-integrity claims' },
  { key: 'data-curation', path: 'src/agents/data_curation/agent.py', focus: 'real logic vs stub; AGENT.md alignment; anomaly/codebook handling' },
  { key: 'data-steward', path: 'src/agents/data_steward/agent.py', focus: 'THE test-split firewall — get_split_paths gate checks (Gate2 AND Gate3), _assert_seed_unchanged, seed immutability; AGENT.md behavioral constraints; any way to get test paths without both gates' },
  { key: 'embedding-agent', path: 'src/agents/embedding_agent/agent.py', focus: 'real logic vs stub; model/version provenance; data-sovereignty (embeddings never leave host); AGENT.md alignment' },
  { key: 'hypothesis-formalizer', path: 'src/agents/hypothesis_formalizer/agent.py', focus: 'PROTECTED; HARKing prevention; FormalHypothesis shape (hypothesis_id), EXP-vs-confirmatory separation; produces the Gate-2 hypothesis_set; SAP §1 alignment' },
  { key: 'p1-strm-nlp', path: 'src/agents/p1_strm_nlp/agent.py', focus: 'PROTECTED; EXP_ prefix; no p-value language; run_confirmatory test-split access guard; SAP/AGENT.md alignment' },
  { key: 'p2-control-topology', path: 'src/agents/p2_control_topology/agent.py', focus: 'PROTECTED; EXP_ prefix; NIST-cluster constraint (NIST 800-53/CSF/800-171 as ONE source); no p-value language; test-split guard' },
  { key: 'p3-regulatory-convergence', path: 'src/agents/p3_regulatory_convergence/agent.py', focus: 'PROTECTED; EXP_ prefix; no p-value language; test-split guard; AGENT.md/SAP alignment' },
  { key: 'p4-risk-blindspot', path: 'src/agents/p4_risk_blindspot/agent.py', focus: 'PROTECTED; false-coverage exclusion (controls without STRM mappings excluded from blindspot claims); EXP_ prefix; no p-value language; test-split guard' },
  { key: 'p5-ai-governance', path: 'src/agents/p5_ai_governance/agent.py', focus: 'PROTECTED; UMAP params pre-registered; EXP_ prefix; no p-value language; test-split guard' },
  { key: 'statistical-analyst', path: 'src/agents/statistical_analyst/agent.py', focus: 'PROTECTED; confirmatory only; multiple-comparison correction per SAP §6; SAP header; decision rules; null-result integrity; test-split firewall' },
  { key: 'repo-documentation', path: 'src/agents/repo_documentation/agent.py', focus: 'executive-mode gating (only after Gate 4); branding compliance; no SCF-derived data leaves machine (CC BY-ND); AGENT.md alignment' },
]

const NODE_UNITS = [
  { key: 'data-split-node', path: 'src/nodes/data_split.py', focus: 'SHA-256 seeded determinism, ratio correctness (70/15/15), stratification, idempotency (data_split_verified), no leakage between splits, seed provenance' },
  { key: 'gate-coordinator', path: 'src/nodes/gate_coordinator.py', focus: 'single-writer for gate_status; record completeness; merge-reducer safety' },
]

const CROSS_UNITS = [
  { key: 'xc-firewall-consistency', path: 'cross-cutting: base.assert_gate2_approved vs data_steward.get_split_paths vs every P-agent run_confirmatory vs statistical_analyst', focus: 'Is the test-split firewall enforced CONSISTENTLY and completely across ALL access paths? Can any agent reach test data with only Gate 2 when Gate 3 is also required (or vice versa)? Is assert_gate2_approved sufficient, or a weaker guard than the data steward? Trace every path that could read test_ids/test split.' },
  { key: 'xc-protected-flags', path: 'cross-cutting: PROTECTED/IMPLEMENTED/AGENT_ID across all 13 agents', focus: 'Per base.py + ADR-0015 §77, P1-P5 + statistical-analyst + hypothesis-formalizer MUST be PROTECTED=True; pipeline agents MUST be PROTECTED=False. Verify each agent sets the right flags, IMPLEMENTED matches reality (all stubs => False), AGENT_ID matches the AGENT.md name. Report any wrong/missing flag (Agent Evolution could then modify a protected agent).' },
  { key: 'xc-research-integrity', path: 'cross-cutting: EXP_ prefix, no-p-value-in-exploratory, SAP header on confirmatory, HARKing', focus: 'Across all exploratory agents and the hypothesis formalizer: are the documented integrity rules actually enforceable/enforced in the code paths that exist, or only asserted in docstrings? Any place exploratory output could carry decision language, or confirmatory could run without a SAP header, or hypotheses could be added post-EDA (HARKing)?' },
]

const ALL = [...AGENT_UNITS, ...NODE_UNITS, ...CROSS_UNITS]

phase('Audit')
const confirmed = await pipeline(
  ALL,
  (u) => agent(
    `${CONTEXT}\n\n=== YOUR UNIT: ${u.key} ===\nTarget: ${u.path}\nFocus: ${u.focus}\n\nAudit it deeply now. Locate and read the relevant AGENT.md and SAP/protocol sections too, not just the code.`,
    { label: `audit:${u.key}`, phase: 'Audit', schema: FINDINGS, agentType: 'Explore' }
  ),
  (audit) => parallel((audit?.findings || []).map((f) => () =>
    agent(
      `Adversarial verifier, HC-GRC agent/pipeline deep audit. Repo ${REPO}. REFUTE this candidate by reading the real code AND the cited spec. Remember: an intentional Phase-0/1 stub (NotImplementedError + IMPLEMENTED=False) is NOT a defect — set is_stub_by_design=true and confirmed=false for those. Default confirmed=false unless it is a genuine defect in implemented logic, a real research-integrity hole, or a real code-vs-spec contradiction.\n\n` +
      `CANDIDATE (unit ${audit.unit}):\ntitle: ${f.title}\nseverity: ${f.severity}\nlocation: ${f.location}\nproblem: ${f.problem}\nspec_or_invariant: ${f.spec_or_invariant}\nproposed_fix: ${f.proposed_fix}\n\n` +
      `Verify against actual code + spec (read-only). Cite file:line and the exact spec clause. Be honest about severity.`,
      { label: `verify:${f.title.slice(0, 36)}`, phase: 'Verify', schema: VERDICT, agentType: 'Explore' }
    ).then((v) => ({ ...v, unit: audit.unit, original_severity: f.severity, location: f.location, proposed_fix: f.proposed_fix }))
  ))
)

phase('Synthesize')
const all = confirmed.flat().filter(Boolean)
const real = all.filter((v) => v.confirmed && !v.is_stub_by_design && v.adjusted_severity !== 'none')
const bySev = (s) => real.filter((v) => v.adjusted_severity === s).length
log(`Agent/pipeline deep audit: ${all.length} candidates verified; ${real.length} genuine ` +
    `(${bySev('critical')}C/${bySev('high')}H/${bySev('medium')}M/${bySev('low')}L); ` +
    `${all.filter(v => v.is_stub_by_design).length} were stub-by-design (not defects)`)
return {
  candidates_verified: all.length,
  genuine_count: real.length,
  breakdown: { critical: bySev('critical'), high: bySev('high'), medium: bySev('medium'), low: bySev('low') },
  genuine: real,
  stub_by_design: all.filter((v) => v.is_stub_by_design).map((v) => v.title),
  rejected: all.filter((v) => !v.confirmed && !v.is_stub_by_design).map((v) => ({ title: v.title, why: v.verification.slice(0, 200) })),
}

genuine.title: Documentation ambiguity: assert_gate2_approved docstring vs. protocol spec
genuine.verification:
VERIFIED CONTRADICTION FOUND:

Base.py (101-116) function docstring states: "This is the primary enforcement mechanism for the pre-registration firewall" and only checks Gate 2.

However, the actual test-split access control architecture requires BOTH Gate 2 AND Gate 3:

SPEC EVIDENCE:
1. GATES.md §108 defines Gate 3 as "Analysis Review" which "Requires before approval: All pre-specified confirmatory tests complete on test split" (GATES.md:111). This implies confirmatory tests must run BEFORE Gate 3 approval, but the data steward controls when they CAN run.

2. Data Steward AGENT.md §78 explicitly states: "Never provide test-split access to any agent before Gate 3 confirmation is recorded in the lab notebook. Gate 2 (SAP lock) is a prerequisite but not sufficient — EDA review (Gate 3) must also be confirmed before the test split is unlocked."

3. Data Steward AGENT.md §73 (Handoffs): "Statistical Analyst (train + val before Gate 3, test split unlocked after Gate 3 EDA review — Gate 2 SAP lock is prerequisite)"

4. Data Steward AGENT.md §55 (Outputs): "Test split | ... Locked — read-accessible only after Gate 3 (EDA review complete; Gate 2 SAP lock is prerequisite)"

CODE EVIDENCE:
5. DataStewardAgent.get_split_paths() (agent.py:112-157) IMPLEMENTS the actual enforcement with TWO gates checked (lines 131-152):
   - First checks Gate 2 (line 134-141)
   - Then checks Gate 3 (line 143-152), raising SAPViolationError if not approved
   - The Gate 3 error message explicitly cites AGENT.md: "See agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md — 'Never provide test-split access to any agent before Gate 3 confirmation.'"

CONTRADICTION:
- assert_gate2_approved() checks only Gate 2 (base.py:109-116)
- get_split_paths() checks both Gate 2 AND Gate 3 (agent.py:134-152)
- The docstring at base.py:102-106 claims Gate 2 is "the primary enforcement mechanism" but this is incomplete per spec

04_methods_scaffolding.md §26 states: "All pre-Gate 2 evaluation runs... use train + validation only" — this is true but does NOT say test is locked until Gate 3. However, the implemented spec (AGENT.md and the actual code) makes Gate 3 the unlock point.

CRITICAL FINDING:
This is a genuine spec-code-docs misalignment. The docstring is not merely incomplete — it's actively misleading because assert_gate2_approved() is called in BaseResearchAgent.__call__() (base.py:158) as the SOLE gate check before confirmatory analysis. For research agents that inherit from BaseResearchAgent and don't call get_split_paths(), Gate 3 checking is NOT performed — only Gate 2 is checked. This creates a real vulnerability if a research agent directly accesses state["test_ids"] or calls some other data access method that bypasses get_split_paths().
genuine.recommended_action:
RECOMMENDED FIX (Priority: HIGH):

1. **Update base.py docstring (lines 102-106)** to clarify:
   - assert_gate2_approved() enforces Gate 2 ONLY
   - It unlocks confirmatory phase but test-set data access requires additional control via get_split_paths()
   - Example revision: "Raise SAPViolationError if Gate 2 has not been approved. Confirms readiness for confirmatory phase. However, physical test-split data access is controlled at the data layer (DataStewardAgent.get_split_paths), which enforces both Gate 2 AND Gate 3 approval. This function does NOT enforce Gate 3."

2. **Add defensive check in BaseResearchAgent.run_confirmatory() docstring** (base.py:175-183) to warn: "Agents that access test split data directly (not via DataStewardAgent.get_split_paths) must additionally verify Gate 3 approval before data access."

3. **Better solution: Move full enforcement into a single, unambiguous location** — either:
   - Have all data access go through get_split_paths() (currently a stub), or
   - Create a new helper assert_gate2_gate3_approved() that checks both gates and is called in BaseResearchAgent before run_confirmatory()

4. **Document the control flow clearly** in CLAUDE.md or architecture docs to show: BaseResearchAgent.__call__() checks Gate 2 → calls run_confirmatory() → agent must get data via get_split_paths() which checks Gate 3.

The candidate's proposed fix is sound: clarify that assert_gate2_approved() is incomplete and Gate 3 is needed. However, the better long-term fix is to move the full check into a single enforced location (as per "Or, better: move the full Gate 2+3 check into assert_gate2_approved()") to eliminate the split enforcement point.
genuine.proposed_fix: Update the docstring at base.py:102-106 to clarify that this function enforces Gate 2 ONLY, and that Gate 3 enforcement is the responsibility of data-access layers (specifically DataStewardAgent.get_split_paths()). Additionally, add a comment that for agents that do NOT call get_split_paths() (i.e., agents with alternative data access patterns), a separate Gate 3 check may be needed. Or, better: move the full Gate 2+3 check into assert_gate2_approved() and update all docstrings to reflect that.

genuine.title: Spec contradiction: GATES.md §105 vs. data-steward AGENT.md on test split access timing
genuine.verification:
VERIFIED CONTRADICTION. Three independent sources in the spec and code all confirm that the Data Steward enforces test split access only after BOTH Gate 2 AND Gate 3 approval, contradicting GATES.md line 105's claim that Gate 2 alone makes test split accessible.

Evidence:
1. GATES.md:105 states "Data access change: Test split becomes accessible" at Gate 2
2. data-steward/AGENT.md:78 explicitly states "Never provide test-split access...before Gate 3 confirmation...Gate 2 (SAP lock) is a prerequisite but not sufficient"
3. data-steward/AGENT.md:35 and :55 both confirm "Test split access unlocked at Gate 3"
4. src/nodes/gates.py:310-311 (gate_3_node docstring) states "DataStewardAgent requires BOTH gate_2 AND gate_3 approved before test split access"
5. Implementation: data-steward/AGENT.md:73 lists handoff "test split unlocked after Gate 3 EDA review — Gate 2 SAP lock is prerequisite"

This is internally consistent spec documentation describing a two-gate unlock mechanism, but GATES.md incorrectly singles out Gate 2 as the unlock point, when the actual system requires Gate 3 confirmation.
genuine.recommended_action: GATES.md §105 must be clarified to match the implemented dual-gate requirement. Proposed fix aligns with the candidate's suggestion: clarify that Gate 2 releases the decryption key but Gate 3 (via Data Steward) releases operational access paths. The three documentation sources (GATES.md, data-steward AGENT.md, gates.py) must converge on a single truth statement about test split access timing. Given the implementation enforces Gate 3, GATES.md §105 is the spec error that needs correction.
genuine.proposed_fix: Clarify GATES.md §105 to state: 'Data access change: Test split decryption key released to Orchestrator at Gate 2; however, the Data Steward withholds access paths until Gate 3. This ensures no confirmatory analysis can run before EDA review is complete.' This resolves the contradiction by acknowledging that Gate 2 releases the technical key but Gate 3 releases the operational access.

genuine.title: Missing ara_artifact_paths in _build_prov_record
genuine.verification:
VERIFIED via code inspection:

1. AcquisitionResult class (src/agents/data_acquisition/agent.py:26-58) defines ara_artifact_paths as a required field in __slots__ (line 36), __init__ parameter (line 48), and assignment (line 57).

2. DataAcquisitionAgent._build_prov_record method (lines 142-157) returns a dict with 9 fields: activity, agent_id, run_id, source_url, github_commit_hash, sha256_hash, timestamp_utc, control_count, strm_mapping_count, dvc_remote_confirmed. The ara_artifact_paths field is conspicuously absent.

3. Precedent from DataCurationAgent._build_prov_record (src/agents/data_curation/agent.py:126-140) includes lineage_path (line 139), demonstrating that artifact paths CAN and SHOULD be included in prov records.

4. AGENT.md evaluation criteria (line 91): "ARA structured artifacts pass schema validation — zero validation errors" and outputs table (line 48): "ARA structured artifacts | ara/artifacts/ | JSON + Parquet" establish that these are critical outputs.

5. Per PROVENANCE_MODEL.md (PROV-DM semantics), entities (artifacts) are documented in provenance via WasGeneratedBy relations. While the relation is separate from the activity record in PROV-DM theory, concrete implementation precedent (DataCurationAgent.lineage_path) shows paths should be in the activity record.

The AcquisitionResult class was deliberately designed to carry ara_artifact_paths, but the _build_prov_record method fails to include it. This is not a stub issue (the method is implemented, not raising NotImplementedError) but an incomplete implementation.
genuine.recommended_action: Add ara_artifact_paths to the _build_prov_record return dict at line 156. The proposed fix is correct: insert "ara_artifact_paths": result.ara_artifact_paths, after the dvc_remote_confirmed line. This will ensure the provenance record documents all artifacts produced by the acquisition run, matching the data design intent evident in AcquisitionResult and the pattern set by DataCurationAgent.
genuine.proposed_fix:
Add ara_artifact_paths to the return dict: change line 156 from `"dvc_remote_confirmed": result.dvc_remote_confirmed,` to `"dvc_remote_confirmed": result.dvc_remote_confirmed,
            "ara_artifact_paths": result.ara_artifact_paths,` to include the artifact file paths in the provenance record.

genuine.title: Pipeline stage ordering misalignment with AGENT.md spec
genuine.verification: DEFECT CONFIRMED. Code at src/nodes/phase1.py:145-150 executes DataCurationAgent before DataStewardAgent, contradicting agents/02-data/data-curation/AGENT.md line 22 ("Runs after Data Steward validation") and the workflow diagram (lines 24-34) showing steward-validated splits flowing TO curation. The spec input contract (line 40) explicitly requires "Validated splits" from Data Steward. Both agent implementations confirm this expectation (src/agents/data_curation/agent.py:67 docstring states "validated splits from DataStewardAgent"). The stages list in phase1.py is fully implemented orchestration logic (not a stub), while the agents themselves are Phase 1 stubs. The ordering defect is latent and will cause data integrity violations once agents are implemented — curation will receive unvalidated raw data instead of steward-validated splits.
genuine.recommended_action: Reorder the stages list in data_pipeline_node (src/nodes/phase1.py:145-150) to place DataStewardAgent before DataCurationAgent: [(acquisition, 'data-acquisition'), (steward, 'data-steward'), (curation, 'data-curation'), (embedding, 'embedding-agent')]. Also correct the topology diagram in the module docstring (lines 8-9) to reflect the correct order. This fix must be completed before Phase 1 agent implementations go live, as the current ordering will cause silent data governance violations.
genuine.proposed_fix: Reorder the stages list in data_pipeline_node to place DataStewardAgent before DataCurationAgent: [(acquisition, 'data-acquisition'), (steward, 'data-steward'), (curation, 'data-curation'), (embedding, 'embedding-agent')]

genuine.title: Incomplete documentation of Gate 3 requirement in base.py — docstring contradicts data-steward AGENT.md spec
genuine.verification:
CONFIRMED: The module docstring in /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/src/agents/base.py lines 18-20 states "Confirmatory phase: access test_ids — only after Gate 2 approval" which contradicts the authoritative spec in /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md line 78: "Never provide test-split access to any agent before Gate 3 confirmation is recorded in the lab notebook. Gate 2 (SAP lock) is a prerequisite but not sufficient — EDA review (Gate 3) must also be confirmed before the test split is unlocked."

The function assert_gate2_approved() at lines 101-116 correctly checks only Gate 2 and its docstring accurately describes its scope. However, BaseResearchAgent.__call__() at line 158 invokes only assert_gate2_approved() before calling run_confirmatory(), creating a false impression that Gate 2 alone is the complete guard.

The code enforcement IS correct: DataStewardAgent.get_split_paths() at lines 131-152 properly checks BOTH gate_2 AND gate_3 before returning test split paths. The gate_3_node() docstring at line 310 explicitly states: "DataStewardAgent requires BOTH gate_2 AND gate_3 approved before test split access."

The defect is DOCUMENTATION-ONLY: the module docstring (line 18) and the function docstring (line 102) do not clarify that Gate 2 is foundational, but actual test-split access requires Gate 3 approval downstream in DataStewardAgent. This creates maintenance risk and potential confusion for future developers.
genuine.recommended_action: Update /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/src/agents/base.py module docstring line 18 from "Confirmatory phase: access test_ids — only after Gate 2 approval" to "Confirmatory phase: access test_ids — after Gate 2 approval (prerequisite); test-split access also requires Gate 3 approval (checked by DataStewardAgent at point of actual access)". Update assert_gate2_approved() docstring to clarify: "Raise SAPViolationError if Gate 2 has not been approved. This is the foundational SAP firewall, but test-split access also requires Gate 3 approval (EDA review), which is verified downstream in DataStewardAgent.get_split_paths(). See agents/02-data/data-steward/AGENT.md line 78."
genuine.proposed_fix: Update base.py lines 18-20 docstring to clarify that test-split access requires BOTH Gate 2 AND Gate 3 (not Gate 2 alone). Optionally refactor assert_gate2_approved() to assert_gate2_and_gate3_approved() or update its docstring to explicitly state that callers must independently verify Gate 3 approval. The data-steward.agent.py implementation is correct; this is a documentation and contract defect in base.py that creates future confusion risk.

genuine.title: Docstring describes incorrect Phase 1 evaluation criterion (F1 scores vs. benchmark composites)
genuine.verification: CONFIRMED DEFECT: The docstring at src/agents/embedding_agent/agent.py:109-111 states "Phase 1 runs all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) and records F1 scores to inform Gate 2 model selection." This contradicts SAP §10 (lines 159-170) which specifies: Phase 1 evaluation uses (a) STS-B/SICK-R benchmarks, (b) regulatory domain benchmarks, (c) SCF structural metadata anchor pairs with selection criterion of "composite of Spearman correlation on benchmark sets and anchor consistency score" producing a "ranked shortlist of 3–5 models." Phase 2 (not Phase 1) performs validation split evaluation using Silhouette score, Anchor consistency score, and Cross-model agreement to select the primary model. F1 score (line 121-122) is a performance threshold (0.70) triggering fine-tuning, not a model selection criterion. The docstring misrepresents both what Phase 1 evaluates and what Gate 2 uses for model selection.
genuine.recommended_action: Revise lines 109-111 to read: "Phase 1 evaluates all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) on benchmarks (STS-B, SICK-R, regulatory domain) and SCF structural metadata anchor pairs (STRM '= Equal To' and '∅ No Relation' pairs per SAP §10) using composite Spearman correlation and anchor consistency scores to produce a ranked shortlist for Phase 2 calibration." Add clarification at lines 121-123 that F1 evaluation is a separate performance quality gate (not model selection) executed post-Gate 2 and triggering domain fine-tuning if F1 < 0.70.
genuine.proposed_fix: Revise line 110-111 to accurately describe Phase 1: 'Phase 1 evaluates all 5 candidates (3 general + 2 diversity anchors) on benchmarks and SCF structural metadata anchor pairs (per SAP §10 Phase 1) to produce a ranked shortlist for Phase 2 calibration.' Then clarify the post-Phase-2 F1 evaluation in line 121-122 as part of the Gate 2 decision process.

genuine.title: Docstring return type for prov_activities is imprecise across all data pipeline agents
genuine.verification: VERIFIED. HCGRCState.prov_activities is Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b] (src/state.py:173). Actual implementations return {'prov_activities': [record]} (src/nodes/data_split.py:168-178, src/nodes/orchestrator.py). Phase1 node checks isinstance(v, list) and extends (src/nodes/phase1.py:165-166). Tests iterate over prov_activities as list (test_orchestrator_grounding.py:22). genuine.title: EXP_ prefix convention lacks code-level enforcement
genuine.verification: Read base.py:22-26, 168-172; p1_strm_nlp/agent.py:15,78; gates.py:320; phase1.py:232; state.py:141-145; and 04_methods_scaffolding.md Section 2. Found zero code-based validation of EXP_ prefix. Docstrings assert the convention; Gate 3 checklist references it; no agent has a wrapper validating artifact filenames before write. The core exploratory/confirmatory firewall (data split via assert_gate2_approved) IS technically enforced, but the EXP_ prefix convention relies on human review only. All P-agents currently raise NotImplementedError.
genuine.recommended_action: Implement a base method BaseResearchAgent._validate_artifact_path(path: str) -> str that: (1) checks if phase is exploratory and filename lacks EXP_ prefix, (2) raises ArtifactNamingError (new exception class), (3) logs to prov_activities. Call this in run_exploratory before returning eda_artifacts in the state update dict. Document this as a Phase 1 hardening requirement before real artifact writes begin. This protects against misconfiguration and strengthens the audit trail without requiring Gate 3 operator vigilance for a mechanical check.
genuine.proposed_fix: Wrap all agent artifact-write operations through a validation layer that: (1) intercepts file write calls (parquet, json, csv, markdown), (2) checks that exploratory-phase writes use EXP_ prefix, (3) logs violations to both provenance and a 'integrity_violations' state field, (4) raises IntegrityViolationError to halt execution if a non-compliant filename is attempted. Implement this in a base method on BaseResearchAgent that validates artifact paths before returning them in the state update dict.

genuine.title: Gate 3 checklist contradicts SAP on data-adaptive hypothesis selection and mitigations
genuine.verification: CONFIRMED — Read actual code at /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/src/nodes/gates.py:342-343 and SAP at /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/docs/protocol/03_statistical_analysis_plan.md:190-202. The Gate 3 checklist item states: "Effect size thresholds are grounded in domain literature — not selected to match the EDA distribution (that would be HARKing)". However, SAP §12 explicitly states (line 192): "hypotheses entering Gate 2 are selected based on what looked tractable or promising in the exploratory phase. This is not traditional pre-registration. It is a two-stage adaptive design with a registration checkpoint between stages." The SAP specifies THREE mitigations (lines 196, 198, 200): (1) Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction, (2) mandatory null result reporting, (3) epistemic status labeling with `data_adaptive`/`theory_derived`/`exploratory` tags. The Gate 3 checklist item fails to require verification of ANY of these mitigations. Cross-check of implementation: FormalHypothesis dataclass (hypothesis_formalizer/agent.py) and ConfirmatoryResult dataclass (statistical_analyst/agent.py) show NO epistemic_status or data_adaptive fields. Both agents are NotImplementedError stubs, so verification impossible at runtime. The checklist item is misleading because it asserts effect sizes are "not selected to match EDA distribution" when the entire design IS data-adaptive selection, controlled by the three mitigations that the checklist does not mention.
genuine.recommended_action: Revise Gate 3 checklist item (gates.py line 342-343) from the current single-sentence statement to a three-part verification requirement: (1) Verify all hypotheses are marked in PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md as either `theory-derived` (pre-EDA literature) or `data-adaptive` (post-EDA exploratory findings); (2) Confirm SAP §6 specifies Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction will be applied across all confirmatory hypotheses, regardless of origin; (3) Verify that ConfirmatoryResult records will include `epistemic_status` field populated with `theory_derived`, `data_adaptive`, or `exploratory` for every finding in all outputs. This aligns the checklist with the three-mitigation strategy documented in SAP §12:194-202 and makes explicit the project's legitimate adaptive design rather than falsely implying traditional pre-specification.
genuine.proposed_fix: Revise Gate 3 checklist line 343 from 'Effect size thresholds are grounded in domain literature — not selected to match the EDA distribution (that would be HARKing)' to: 'Effect size thresholds are grounded in domain literature (pre-EDA) OR justified by domain considerations AND the hypothesis is marked 'data_adaptive' in the PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER. All data-adaptive hypotheses receive Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction and 'data_adaptive' epistemic status labeling in reporting.' This aligns the checklist with the SAP §12 design intent.

genuine.title: State reducer permits silent hypothesis modification post-Gate-2 without amendment audit trail
genuine.verification: The defect is real but currently mitigated by agent-level controls. The `_merge_hypotheses()` reducer in src/state.py:55-71 deduplicates hypotheses by hypothesis_id with "latest wins" semantics, allowing any node to silently replace a hypothesis post-Gate-2 without enforcement. This violates the spec constraint in agents/01-research/hypothesis-formalizer/AGENT.md line 73 requiring "formal amendment with amendment ID, original text, revised text, reason, date" for post-Gate-2 modifications. The PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md (lines 71-80) defines the formal `sap-amendment` entry type with required justification. Currently, HypothesisFormalizerAgent.run_confirmatory() raises SAPViolationError (src/agents/hypothesis_formalizer/agent.py:114) blocking the agent itself, but the state reducer has no Gate 2 guard, creating a vulnerability if Phase 2 agents bypass the agent-level control. No `hypothesis_registration_lock` field exists in HCGRCState. The vulnerability exists in the state architecture, not the current agent implementation.
genuine.recommended_action: Add state-level enforcement: (1) Create `hypothesis_registration_lock: str | None` field in HCGRCState initialized to None; (2) At Gate 2 approval, compute SHA-256 hash of full hypothesis_set and store in hypothesis_registration_lock; (3) Before any hypothesis_set update post-Gate-2, check if hypothesis_registration_lock is not None and phase=="phase_2" — if so, verify the update includes an explicit `sap_amendment_id` field and compare hashes; (4) Raise SAPViolationError in the reducer if post-Gate-2 mutation is attempted without amendment metadata. This shifts enforcement from the agent layer to the state machine layer, making violation impossible by design rather than relying on agent discipline.
genuine.proposed_fix: Add a 'hypothesis_registration_lock' state field that: (1) at Gate 2 approval, captures a SHA-256 hash of the full hypothesis_set, (2) before any hypothesis merge, check if we're in phase_2 (post-Gate-2), (3) if so, forbid any change to hypothesis_set EXCEPT through a formal amendment process (which requires an explicit 'sap_amendment' entry in hypothesis update with amendment_id, original_hash, new_hash), (4) if someone tries to slip in a modified hypothesis post-Gate-2 without amendment, raise SAPViolationError. Enforce at the state reducer level.

genuine.title: Exploratory_complete flag logic permits partial EDA; violates state.py contract
genuine.verification: Read /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/src/nodes/phase1.py lines 292-294: exploratory_complete = any_complete and not all_stubs allows True when 1 of 5 agents complete (others are stub_pending). Verified state.py line 130 docstring explicitly specifies: "True after all P1-P5 nodes complete (or graceful stub)". Verified gates.py line 231-233 uses binary flag for Gate 2 hard prerequisite without checking individual agent counts. Verified gates.py:315-316 (Gate 3) documents intended semantics: "exploratory_complete must be True (or all agents in stub_pending status acceptable for dry-run)" — explicitly mentioning the all-stubs exception. The formula contradicts both the schema contract (line 130) and the gate 3 semantics (must be all OR all-stubs, not mixed).
genuine.recommended_action: Change phase1.py line 294 to: exploratory_complete = all_complete_or_all_stubs where all_complete = all(s["status"] in ("completed", "failed") for s in agent_statuses if s["status"] != "stub_pending") or all agents count == 5. Alternatively, add explicit check at Gate 2 line 231-233: verify all agents either "completed" or "stub_pending" (no partial implementation allowed). Add test case where 1 agent completes + 4 are stub_pending, assert exploratory_complete=False.
genuine.proposed_fix: Clarify Gate 2 checklist line 232: change 'exploratory_complete is False — P1-P5 exploratory analysis has not finished' to list a specific minimum requirement, e.g., 'all five P1-P5 modules must have completed (status='completed') OR explicitly deferred in eda_agent_statuses'. Alternatively, add a state field 'min_modules_required' set to 5, and check 'at least min_modules_required agents completed' rather than 'at least one completed'.
